world.title: Thailand: reform deferred again
world.description: The conservative Bhumjaithai Party won Thailand’s 8 February election, while the progressive People’s Party lost seats. A simmering border conflict with Cambodia helped shape voters’ choice. The People’s Party, which seeks to rein in the military and monarchy’s power, has also been weakened by systematic repression. The result is a setback for the hopes of economic, social and political reform that drove a youth-led protest movement in recent years, many of whose leaders have been criminalised. One potential positive is that a referendum saw voters back the start of a process to develop a new constitution.

world.description: A total of 32 political parties set to participate in the direct polls

world.description: Trump's threat was so broad it did not seem to account for the harm to civilians, prompting some to say such strikes would violate international law.

world.title: Markets live updates: ASX rallies, oil falls back below $US100 on Donald Trump's Iran strike suspension
world.description: The US president announced a two-week ceasefire, as the clock counted down to his Iran deadline. The ASX has jumped on the news, while oil futures have plunged about 15pc. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

world.description: There's a phenomena called Lazarus taxa, where living organisms that were at one point declared extinct are later rediscovered alive, like the coelacanth fish

business.title: Delta raises checked baggage fees as the Iran war drives up jet fuel prices
business.description: Delta Air lines is joining a growing list of U.S. carriers raising checked bag fees. The move announced Tuesday comes as higher fuel costs ripple through the airline industry.

science.title: African frogs haven't forgotten the ice ages. Scientists can tell by where they live.
science.description: Why are frogs diverse in some parts of Africa's rainforests and less so in others? The patterns of cooling and glaciation during the last ice age would probably not have been your first answer or even your last-ditch guess, but it is, nonetheless, correct.

science.title: Key protein required for the function and survival of pheromone‑sensing neurons identified
science.description: Researchers at the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), Hyderabad have identified a mammalian protein, Cnpy1 (Canopy1), that is essential for the survival and function of vomeronasal sensory neurons in mice. Published in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, the study shows that Cnpy1 acts as a specialized endoplasmic‑reticulum‑associated factor required to maintain functional receptor complexes in these neurons, allowing them to thrive in an unusually high ER‑stress‑like environment.

science.title: NASA releases first-ever photo of Earth from the far side of the moon
science.description: NASA unveiled the first-ever photo of Earth from the far side of the moon, dubbed "Earthset," captured by Artemis II astronauts during their lunar flyby Monday.

science.title: Researchers clarify how cells remove damaged endoplasmic reticulum
science.description: The cell's endoplasmic reticulum (ER) plays a central role in protein synthesis, folding, and calcium (Ca²⁺) storage. When damaged, ER-phagy (self-eating) removes affected ER regions via double-membrane vesicles called autophagosomes. However, the mechanisms underlying its initiation, signaling, and membrane origin had previously been unclear.

science.title: "Payoff day": Jenni Gibbons on watching Artemis II crew make lunar history
science.description: The Artemis II mission has at times left Jenni Gibbons feeling tense — and tired.  But the sometimes bated breath and fatigue aren’t hindering the Calgary-b…

science.title: Fly ball: Drosophila can learn while playing with tiny spheres
science.description: For more than a century, the fruit fly has been a workhorse of the biological sciences that has helped scientists to make fundamental breakthroughs in fields such as genetics and neuroscience. As it turns out, human scientists are not the only ones learning during these experiments. A recent study by Harvard neuroscientists in Current Biology shows how fruit flies are themselves figuring things out—in this case, how to manipulate balls with "play-like" experimentation.

science.title: What the Artemis II astronauts saw from space : NPR
science.description: The astronauts on Artemis II observed parts of the moon humans had never seen before. Their findings provide a scientific baseline — and sense of wonder — for future missions.

science.title: Metabolic Enzyme Identified as a Selective Vulnerability in APC-Deficient Cancer Cells
science.description: A precision cancer strategy that selectively kills tumour cells while leaving healthy tissue intact may be within reach for the majority of colorectal cancer

ai.title: Elon Musk Gives Intel The AI Foundry Stamp It's Been Chasing
ai.description: Intel just got a public endorsement its foundry ambitions from Elon Musk as it joins the Terafab project with SpaceX, Tesla and xAI.

ai.title: I can't help rooting for tiny open source AI model maker Arcee
ai.description: Arcee is a tiny 26-person U.S. startup that built a high-performing, massive, open source LLM. And it's gaining popularity with OpenClaw users.

ai.title: AI-powered blood test paves the way for early diagnosis of leprosy
ai.description: A new blood test combined with a standardized questionnaire and artificial intelligence may transform the way leprosy is diagnosed in Brazil. Researchers at the University of São Paulo (USP) tested the new test in Brazil using blood samples collected during a COVID-19 population survey. The method showed potential for identifying the disease earlier, in its initial stages, when symptoms are subtle and traditional laboratory tests often fail.

ai.title: Michigan man allegedly used AI to create child sexually abusive material, prosecutors say
ai.description: Artificial intelligence technology was used to create at least some of the more than 40,0000 child sexually abusive images that resulted in multiple felony charges, prosecutors say.

ai.title: VC Eclipse has a new $1.3B to back - and build - ‘physical AI’ startups
ai.description: Eclipse will put some of that money towards incubating, or building startups.

ai.title: Opinion: Heaven help us! Religious groups need to play a role in AI policy
ai.description: AI, unwisely used, erodes values that the world’s faith traditions strive to uphold. Catholic, Jewish and Muslim leaders call for people of faith to speak up

ai.title: The battle for the soul of AI
ai.description: The debate over who controls AI intensifies as ethical concerns clash with commercial and geopolitical interests, raising questions on regulation and accountability.

ai.description: FIIPB sectors are 3 million jobs below the pre-pandemic trend, largely due to AI adoption. Read what investors need to know.
